title: Inappropriate ADH Secretion After Colloid Cyst Surgery; A Possible Situation
author: Chafik Handis
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract    
   We report a case of 71 years 71-year-old man for the surgical management of a third ventricular colloid cyst. the procedure consisted of a transfrontal transventricular resection, with a good postoperative status. After his discharge, the patient consulted for headaches and confusion. Biological investigations revealed hyponatremia that was managed finally with a fluid restriction protocol, and the diagnosis of an inappropriate ADH secretion was retained. the specific parameter of this case is the occurrence of this syndrome after an unusual colloid cyst surgery using a transventricular approach.   
         Keywords:      ADH, Hyponatremia, colloid cyst.
